description:
A closure is a struct: { code_ptr; capture_0; capture_1; ... }. Each
field has a type known at compile time. At every MakeClosure site the
compiler knows the body fn, the captured values, and their types. Same
as constructing any other struct.

When we invoke through the closure pointer (call_indirect (load cl+16)),
we need a Cranelift signature for the indirect call. Nothing forces it
to be the universal (i64 Tagged, i64) tail — it can be (RawInt, i64)
or (f64, i64) as long as the body the pointer leads to has a matching
signature.

Today fz emits one closure-target body per fn with sig
(args:i64..., self:i64, cont:i64) tail, all params Tagged. That was a
Time-To-Working-CPS choice. It is not architecturally required and is
the root cause of every downstream patch I have been chasing:

- halt_bound_fns force on return_repr.
- cont_force_tagged_fnid widening at cont seams.
- The producer/consumer mismatches at every seam where typed-direct
  meets universal-Tagged (modules.fz, fact_5, map_higher_order
  failures all trace here).

The fix is one level up: emit typed closure bodies per narrow spec
(already partially done — closure_target_fns has narrow specs), bind
the right narrow spec's code_ptr at each MakeClosure site, emit typed
call_indirect at each CallClosure site with the sig matching the
closure's known static type. Tagged remains a real ABI variant for
the truly-Any-typed flow (mailbox, polymorphic container, dyn-dispatch
sites).

## DAG (provisional)

22.1: drop the all-Tagged param_reprs force on closure_target_fns.
Per-narrow-spec specs already exist; just stop forcing their reprs.

22.2: per-MakeClosure-site narrow spec binding. Codegen stores the
matching narrow spec's body code_ptr at cl+16.

22.3: typed call_indirect at CallClosure / TailCallClosure. Sig
matches the closure's known static type. Tagged fallback for
Any-typed closures.

22.4: cont seams as a special case of typed closure call. Per-cont
typed sig; producer's Term::Return uses cont's typed sig; entry
harness drops the Tagged round-trip.

22.5: typed halt-cont. Halt is a cont whose body calls
fz_halt_implicit_<type>. Falls out of 22.4 + per-program halt-cont
emission.

22.6: drop the codegen-side closure_target_fns return_repr force.
After 22.1-22.5 land, the force is no longer load-bearing.

## Acceptance

Per-fixture golden CLIF diffs show every closure / cont / halt seam
tag/untag round-trip deleted for typed flows. Closure escapes into
Any-typed flow keep the Tagged fallback.

For add1.fz golden:
- add1_s2: no ishl_imm/bor_imm pair at the cont seam.
- k_2_s3: no sshr_imm at entry.

For higher_order.fz: compose's cont captures stored/loaded raw where
typer narrows.

Test suite: 511 + golden diffs blessed cleanly.

## Supersedes

- fz-ul4.27.21 — folds into 22.4 (cont seams).
- the .21.5 / .21.6 paths I was building — superseded by the
  closure-ABI approach.
title: [EPIC] Typed closure ABI — closures are structs with typed fields
